name: Juniper Mesa Homesite
tagline: 10 Acres of Pinyon-Juniper Woodland Outside Bend, Oregon
description:
Ten acres of high-desert juniper woodland on a mesa east of Bend, Oregon — one of the fastest-growing outdoor recreation towns in the West. The property commands sweeping views of the Cascade Range, from Mt. Bachelor to Broken Top to the Three Sisters.

The terrain is classic Central Oregon — volcanic soil, fragrant juniper, and wide-open sky. A natural flat building pad sits near the center of the property with 360-degree views. Power is at the road and the county has pre-approved well and septic permits. Bend's world-class mountain biking, skiing, and craft beer scene is 20 minutes west.

details.terrain: High-desert juniper mesa with volcanic soil

photos.caption: Sweeping views of the Three Sisters from the property

photos.caption: Spectacular high-desert sunsets

photos.caption: Paved access just 20 minutes from Bend

name: Skagit Valley Farmstead
tagline: 5 Acres of Fertile Valley Floor in Northwest Washington
description:
Five acres of deep, loamy farmland in Washington's legendary Skagit Valley — famous for its tulip fields, seed farms, and some of the richest agricultural soil on the West Coast. The property includes irrigation water rights from the Skagit River system and a solid concrete barn foundation from a 1940s dairy operation.

The valley floor is flat and fully usable, with views of the North Cascades to the east and the San Juan Islands ferry terminal just 30 minutes west. All utilities — electric, water, phone, and natural gas — run along the road. This is turnkey farmland in a region where acreage rarely comes up for sale.

details.utilities: Electric, water, gas, phone at road
details.terrain: Flat valley floor, deep loam soil

photos.alt: Fertile farmland with North Cascades backdrop
photos.caption: Rich Skagit Valley soil with mountain views
The Skagit Valley is famous for its tulip fields
Morning mist rising off the valley floor

name: Mendocino Bluff Parcel
tagline: 1.5 Acres of Coastal Bluff with Ocean Views in Northern California
description:
One and a half acres on a windswept coastal bluff just south of the village of Mendocino, California. The property offers unobstructed views of the Pacific Ocean from a dramatic headland setting — gray whales pass within sight during winter migration. Sea stacks and rocky coves line the shore below.

The lot is one of the last undeveloped parcels within walking distance of Mendocino village, a National Historic Preservation district known for its Victorian architecture, art galleries, and the landmark Mendocino Headlands State Park. Underground electric and community water are stubbed to the lot. Build your dream on one of the most iconic coastlines in America.

details.utilities: Underground electric + community water at lot
details.restrictions: Coastal Commission review for building permits
